title: Woodland Friends Forest Adventure
description: A delightful forest scene featuring five friendly woodland animals (fox, owl, deer, rabbit, and squirrel) for children to color while learning about forest habitats and animals.
instructions: Color the forest animals using your favorite colors. Try to use colors that these animals might have in real life, or be creative with your own special colors!

content.image: A black and white outline drawing of a forest scene with five woodland animals arranged in a balanced composition. The image features a fox sitting near a tree stump, an owl perched on a branch, a deer standing gracefully with its head slightly raised, a rabbit with long ears sitting in grass, and a squirrel holding an acorn. All animals have friendly expressions. The outlines are thick and bold with minimal background elements like simple trees, mushrooms, and a few flowers. Each animal has a blank space near it for a label.
learningFacts: Foxes are clever animals that can run up to 45 miles per hour!
Owls can turn their heads almost all the way around to see behind them.
Male deer grow new antlers every year.
Rabbits have strong back legs that help them hop very fast.
Squirrels collect and store nuts to eat during winter.
